frameworks.name: approximate Kalman filter
frameworks.one_line: tracks trial-by-trial value and estimation uncertainty in a multi-armed bandit

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: framing for how uncertainty shapes learning and choice

core_question: How does uncertainty influence value learning, choice, and overt gaze allocation in bandit decisions?
key_assumptions: trial-by-trial belief is captured by a Kalman-filter-style mean and variance
overt gaze indexes covert processing of value and uncertainty
absolute prediction error is a momentary uncertainty signal
manipulations: multi-armed bandit options with varying value and uncertainty
concurrent recording of choice and gaze allocation
key_findings: estimated value and estimation uncertainty predicted what subjects looked at before choosing
value, uncertainty, and fixation itself influenced choice
absolute prediction error governed how long subjects looked at obtained outcomes
interpretations: uncertainty affects multiple stages of behavior — pre-choice gaze, choice, and post-outcome looking
fixation is both shaped by uncertainty and contributes to the decision itself
